articles.title: Heatwave takes a backseat as rain lashes Delhi-NCR, IMD issues orange alert - CNBC TV18
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Delhi
articles.full_text:
3 Min Read
A spell of rain and thunderstorms swept across Delhi and adjoining National Capital Region (NCR) late on the night of Monday, May 5, bringing much-needed relief from the prevailing heat.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has sounded an orange alert for the city, as per news agency PTI.
Areas including Kashmiri Gate, India Gate, Akshardham, Safdarjung, Nehru Stadium, Defence Colony and Lajpat Nagar were likely to witness intense weather activity.
The department said that an orange alert translates to “be prepared”, signalling the possibility of disruptive weather conditions.

Isolated parts of the city such as Burari, Model Town, Delhi University, Patel Nagar, Vasant Vihar, R K Puram, Hauz Khas and Kalkaji could experience lighter rainfall with thunderstorms and winds of 30–50 kmph.
Rains, thunderstorms across NCR
Neighbouring NCR regions also remain under the influence of the weather system.
Areas like Ghaziabad, Indirapuram, Loni Dehat and Hindon Air Force Station are likely to see similar thunderstorm activity, while Noida, Greater Noida, Gurugram and Faridabad may receive scattered rainfall with lightning, as per PTI.
Delhi NCR weather forecast
Unsettled weather conditions are expected to persist over Delhi in the coming days, the IMD said in its weather bulletin.
On May 5, the city is likely to witness a generally cloudy sky with light rain, thunderstorms and gusty winds reaching 40 kmph during the afternoon or evening. Maximum temperatures are expected to hover between 31-33 degrees Celsius, significantly below normal for this time of year.

For May 6, the IMD has forecast partly cloudy conditions with chances of thundery development later in the day. Temperatures are likely to rise slightly, with maximum readings between 35-37 degrees Celsius.
By May 7, the weather is expected to remain partly cloudy, with temperatures ranging from 36-38 degrees Celsius, though still below seasonal averages.
Wider weather systems at play
The IMD attributed the changing weather pattern to multiple systems, including a western disturbance and cyclonic circulations over north and central India.
These are likely to trigger thunderstorms, lightning and gusty winds across several regions, including northwest and central India, over the next few days.
While rainfall continues to moderate temperatures, the IMD cautioned that fluctuating conditions, including strong winds and lightning, may persist, requiring residents to remain vigilant.

The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has sounded an orange alert for the city, as per news agency PTI.
The IMD warned of thunderstorms accompanied by lightning, light to moderate rainfall and gusty winds reaching speeds of up to 60 kmph at several locations across the capital.
Areas including Kashmiri Gate, India Gate, Akshardham, Safdarjung, Nehru Stadium, Defence Colony and Lajpat Nagar were likely to witness intense weather activity.
The department said that an orange alert translates to “be prepared”, signalling the possibility of disruptive weather conditions.

articles.title: Delhi Weather Alert: Check out Delhi's Weather on 5 May and the Upcoming Heatwave in the Coming Days - NativePlanet
articles.search_term: (heatwave OR "heat wave" OR "severe heatwave" OR "extreme heat" OR "heat spell" OR "scorching heat" OR "blistering heat" OR "sweltering heat" OR "hot winds" OR loo OR "hot loo winds" OR "intense heat" OR "unbearable heat" OR "deadly heat" OR heat OR "weather forecast" OR "weather update" OR "weather alert" OR "IMD forecast" OR "IMD prediction" OR "IMD alert" OR IMD OR "rising temperatures" OR "temperatures expected to rise" OR "relief from heat" OR "respite from heat" OR "heat respite" OR "respite from scorching heat") Delhi
articles.full_text:
Today Delhi's weather is expected to bring a short break from the ongoing heat, as rain and thunderstorms are likely across the city, which will provide some relief after several hot days. However, this comfort will not last long, as temperatures are set to rise again in the coming days, possibly leading to heatwave conditions.
Delhi Weather Alert
The rain and thunderstorms today may lower the temperature slightly, with the maximum expected to be around 33°C. Even though this is cooler than previous days, high humidity levels reaching up to 90% will make the weather feel sticky and uncomfortable. The combination of moisture in the air and warm temperatures will create a feeling of heaviness, especially during the afternoon hours.
Delhi Heat Alert
From May 6 onwards, the temperature is expected to increase steadily. It may rise to around 36°C on May 6 and 37°C on May 7. Between May 8 and May 10, temperatures could reach 38-39°C. As the clouds clear and sunlight becomes stronger, the city will experience more intense heat during the day, adding to people's discomfort. After the brief rainy spell, Delhi may soon face heatwave-like conditions. The drop in humidity and the arrival of dry, hot winds will make the heat more intense. These conditions can be harmful, especially for those who spend long hours outdoors.
Advisory for Residents
People are advised to take precautions to stay safe during the heat. Drinking enough water, avoiding direct sunlight during peak hours, and watching for signs of heat-related problems like tiredness, dizziness, or headaches are important. Extra care should be taken for children and elderly people, as they are more vulnerable to extreme heat.

articles.title: Rain, thunderstorms lash Delhi late at night; IMD places city under orange alert - BusinessLine
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Delhi
articles.full_text:
Rain and thunderstorms lashed the national capital late at Monday night, as the India Meteorological Department placed the city under an orange alert, with hail, lightning and gusty winds likely in the next few hours.
According to the IMD, an orange alert translates to "be prepared," indicating the likelihood of adverse weather conditions.
The weather department said thunderstorms accompanied by light to moderate rainfall and lightning, with wind speeds reaching up to 60 kmph, are very likely to occur at a few places in Delhi, including Kashmiri Gate, India Gate, Akshardham, Safdarjung, Nehru Stadium, Defence Colony and Lajpat Nagar.
It added that light to moderate rainfall accompanied by light thunderstorms and lightning, with winds of 30-50 kmph, is also very likely at isolated places such as Burari, Model Town, Delhi University, Patel Nagar, Vasant Vihar, R K Puram, Vasant Kunj, Hauz Khas, Malviya Nagar, Kalkaji, Mehrauli, Tughlakabad, Chhattarpur, IGNOU and Ayanagar.
In the National Capital Region, similar weather conditions are likely to prevail in areas such as Loni Dehat, Hindon Air Force Station, Ghaziabad, Indirapuram and Chhapraula, while isolated rainfall and thunderstorms are expected in Noida, Greater Noida, Gurugram, Faridabad, Manesar and Ballabhgarh.
Published on May 5, 2026

articles.title: Delhi Metro Under Red Alert: Why Your Evening Commute Might Be A Nightmare Today - NativePlanet
articles.search_term: ("heat advisory" OR "heat action plan" OR "heat alert" OR "IMD warning" OR "IMD alert" OR "red alert" OR "orange alert" OR "yellow alert" OR "NDMA advisory" OR "cooling shelter" OR "relief measures" OR "heat compensation" OR "severe weather warning") Delhi
articles.full_text:
The IMD issued a red alert for Delhi-NCR today. Residents should prepare for severe thunderstorms and hail. These extreme weather conditions will cause delays on Delhi Metro lines. Commuters must plan journeys early to avoid peak hour crowds.
Heavy rain and strong winds will hit the region this afternoon. Experts predict wind speeds could reach seventy kilometers per hour. Such weather forces the Delhi Metro to reduce train speeds. This precaution ensures passenger safety during high winds on elevated tracks.
Delhi Metro Delays Amid Red Alert
DMRC officials may briefly halt trains during intense lightning or hail. These safety protocols are standard during red alert weather events. Reduced speeds often lead to significant bunching of trains at major junctions. This results in longer waiting times for daily office commuters today.
Underground stations might see a sudden surge in passenger numbers. People often seek shelter from heavy rain inside the metro premises. This can lead to congestion at entry and exit gates. Security teams are on high alert to manage these unexpected crowds today.
IMD Red Alert Weather Forecast for NCR
The weather office warns of localized flooding in low-lying areas. Sudden hail storms could also damage temporary structures and affect visibility. Authorities advise people to stay indoors during the peak intensity hours. Local traffic police expect waterlogging to slow down road transport today.
Travelers should monitor the DMRC social media handles for live updates. Official alerts will provide real-time information on specific corridor status. Staying informed helps riders choose alternative routes if some lines face issues. Rapid changes in weather require everyone to remain very cautious.
Managing Commutes During Delhi Metro Delays
Managing a commute during a red alert requires some proactive steps. Travelers should leave at least thirty minutes early for their destination. Check the weather forecast before stepping out of your home or office. Below is a quick summary of the expected impact on transport.
"Safety is our top priority during extreme weather," a transport official noted. They emphasized that speed limits are necessary when winds exceed certain levels. Operating trains at lower speeds prevents sway on high elevated tracks. These measures protect the overhead electrical wires from potential damage.
Avoiding Peak Hour Rush and Red Alert Impact
Major interchange hubs like Rajiv Chowk may experience higher footfall than usual. When trains run slow, platforms can get crowded very quickly. It is best to avoid these stations during the evening rush hour. Use peripheral lines if possible to reach your destination safely.
Road traffic will likely be worse due to poor visibility and rain. Many commuters who usually drive might switch to the Delhi Metro today. This shift adds further pressure on an already strained public transit system. Expect heavy demand for app-based cabs at exit points.
Planning Your Evening Travel During Red Alert
Thunderstorm activity is expected to subside by late tonight or tomorrow. However, the impact on rail services may linger for some time. DMRC technical teams will inspect the tracks once the storm passes over. This ensures the system is ready for normal speeds tomorrow.
Stay safe and keep your mobile phones charged for emergency communication. Follow all instructions given by security personnel at the metro stations. A little patience will help everyone navigate this stormy weather day safely. Check local news for further updates on the Delhi-NCR situation.

articles.title: Delhi implements heatwave measures for DTC buses and workers - The News Mill
articles.full_text:
Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta announced on Monday that the government is fully prepared to manage the ongoing heatwave, issuing clear department-specific guidelines to reduce difficulties for residents. She detailed that all Delhi Transport Corporation (DTC) buses will be equipped with cold water via onboard cool boxes to ensure commuters have access to drinking water whenever required.
Additionally, water counters will be established near bus shelters to provide free cold water and oral rehydration solution (ORS) to the public. The Labour Department has instructed all employers to institute a mandatory rest period for workers between 1 pm and 4 pm to protect them from the peak heat hours. Employers are also responsible for supplying drinking water, shaded areas, and other essential facilities at worksites.
Regarding school safety, the Chief Minister emphasised that children remain a government priority. Schools have been directed to ensure sufficient drinking water, proper ventilation, and heat protection measures. It is now mandatory to provide each student with an ORS drink before dismissal to better protect them during their journey home, according to the government’s press release.
Gupta stated that all departments are tasked with strictly implementing these guidelines and ensuring effective communication with all concerned parties. The Municipal Corporation of Delhi (MCD) and Public Works Department (PWD) have been specifically instructed to safeguard the health of sanitation workers and construction labourers, including the suspension of work during peak afternoon hours.
The health system is on full alert, with hospitals stocked with essential medicines and equipped to handle emergencies. Fire, power, and water departments have been placed on alert to guarantee uninterrupted services throughout the city. All water resources are being fully activated and used optimally to maintain supply.
Regarding fire services, quick response vehicles have been deployed to ensure rapid action at incident sites, alongside ongoing efforts to prevent emergencies. “The government is fully prepared and alert. Our priority is to ensure that people of Delhi face minimal inconvenience during the peak summer months,” Gupta said.

articles.title: दिल्ली-NCR में आज भी बारिश का येलो अलर्ट, IMD का दावा- अगले 7 दिन तक गर्मी के तेवर रहेंगे नरम - Jagran
articles.search_term: ("हीट एक्शन प्लान" OR "गर्मी से राहत" OR चेतावनी OR "रेड अलर्ट" OR "ऑरेंज अलर्ट" OR "येलो अलर्ट" OR एडवाइजरी OR "राहत सामग्री" OR "राहत शिविर" OR मुआवजा) Delhi
articles.full_text:
दिल्ली-एनसीआर में 2 दिन आंधी और बारिश का अलर्ट, IMD का दावा- अगले 7 दिन तक गर्मी के तेवर रहेंगे नरम
दिल्ली-एनसीआर में 5 और 6 मई को बारिश, तेज हवाओं और गरज-चमक का अलर्ट जारी किया गया है। इससे गर्मी से राहत मिलेगी, लेकिन लोगों को अचानक बदलते मौसम के प् ...और पढ़ें
राज्य ब्यूरो, नई दिल्ली। दिल्ली-एनसीआर में पिछले दो दिनों से बदले मौसम का सिलसिला अभी थमने वाला नहीं है। मौसम विभाग के मुताबिक, राजधानी और आसपास के इलाकों में अगले दो दिन यानी 5 और 6 मई तक बारिश, तेज हवाएं और गरज-चमक के आसार बने रहेंगे।
इससे एक ओर जहां लोगों को गर्मी से राहत मिलेगी, वहीं अचानक बदलते मौसम के कारण सावधानी भी जरूरी हो गई है। इसके साथ ही अगले सात दिन तक गर्मी के तेवन नरम ही रहेंगे।
5 मई: बादल, आंधी और बारिश का असर
मंगलवार को दिल्ली-एनसीआर में आसमान में दिनभर बादल छाए रहने की संभावना है। खासतौर पर दोपहर और शाम के समय कई इलाकों में तेज हवाओं के साथ हल्की से मध्यम बारिश हो सकती है। कुछ जगहों पर आंधी और बिजली गिरने की भी चेतावनी दी गई है।
6 मई: बादल रहेंगे, बारिश कम होने के संकेत
बुधवार को भी आसमान में बादल छाए रहेंगे, लेकिन बारिश की गतिविधि थोड़ी कम हो सकती है। हालांकि, उमस और हल्की हवाओं के साथ मौसम अपेक्षाकृत सुहावना बना रहेगा।
7 से 10 मई: बादलों का डेरा जारी
मौसम विभाग के अनुसार, 7 से 10 मई के बीच भी दिल्ली-एनसीआर में आसमान साफ नहीं होगा। बीच-बीच में बादल छाए रहने और कुछ इलाकों में हल्की बारिश की संभावना बनी रहेगी, खासकर 10 मई के आसपास फिर से बारिश सक्रिय हो सकती है।
क्या असर पड़ेगा?
- बिजली गिरने और तेज हवाओं के चलते सतर्क रहने की जरूरत
लोगों के लिए सलाह

articles.title: दिल्ली में देर रात गरज के साथ बारिश; आईएमडी ने ‘ऑरेंज अलर्ट’ जारी किया - IBC24 News
articles.search_term: ("हीट एक्शन प्लान" OR "गर्मी से राहत" OR चेतावनी OR "रेड अलर्ट" OR "ऑरेंज अलर्ट" OR "येलो अलर्ट" OR एडवाइजरी OR "राहत सामग्री" OR "राहत शिविर" OR मुआवजा) Delhi
articles.full_text:
Delhi Weather Update: राजधानी वासियों को गर्मी से मिलेगी राहत, अगले कुछ घंटों में तेज आंधी-बारिश का अनुमान, मौसम विभाग ने जारी किया अलर्ट
Delhi Weather Update: राजधानी वासियों को गर्मी से मिलेगी राहत, अगले कुछ घंटों में तेज आंधी-बारिश का अनुमान, मौसम विभाग ने जारी किया अलर्ट
- दिल्ली में गरज-बारिश और ओलावृष्टि की संभावना, ऑरेंज अलर्ट जारी
- कुछ इलाकों में हवाओं की गति 60 किमी/घंटा तक पहुंच सकती है
- मौसम विभाग ने लोगों को सतर्क रहने की अपील की
नयी दिल्ली: Delhi Weather Update राष्ट्रीय राजधानी में सोमवार देर रात गरज के साथ बारिश हुई। मौसम विभाग ने ‘ऑरेंज अलर्ट’ जारी किया है और अगले कुछ घंटों में दिल्ली में ओलावृष्टि, बिजली गिरने और तेज हवाएं चलने का अनुमान है। भारत मौसम विज्ञान विभाग (आईएमडी) के अनुसार, ‘ऑरेंज अलर्ट’ का मतलब है ‘‘सतर्क रहें’’, जो प्रतिकूल मौसम की संभावना को दर्शाता है।
Delhi Weather Update मौसम विभाग ने कहा कि कश्मीरी गेट, इंडिया गेट, अक्षरधाम, सफदरजंग, जवाहरलाल नेहरू स्टेडियम, डिफेंस कॉलोनी और लाजपत नगर सहित दिल्ली के कुछ स्थानों पर हल्की से मध्यम बारिश और बिजली गिरने के साथ बारिश होने का अनुमान है, जिसमें हवा की गति 60 किलोमीटर प्रति घंटे तक पहुंच सकती है।
मौसम विभाग ने कहा कि बुराड़ी, मॉडल टाउन, दिल्ली विश्वविद्यालय, पटेल नगर, वसंत विहार, आर के पुरम, वसंत कुंज, हौज खास, मालवीय नगर, कालकाजी, महरौली, तुगलकाबाद, छतरपुर, इग्नू और आयानगर जैसे कुछ स्थानों पर हल्की से मध्यम बारिश, हल्की गरज और बिजली गिरने का अनुमान है। साथ ही हवा की गति 30-50 किलोमीटर प्रति घंटा तक रह सकती है।

- Himanta Biswa Sarma On Pawan Khera: ‘मैं कुछ बोलता हूं तो पीछे नहीं हटता, पेड़ा जरूर बनेगा’, चुनाव जीतने के बाद हिमंत बिस्वा सरमा ने इस नेता पर साधा निशाना
- West Bengal Election Results 2026: बंगाल में दीदी के साथ हो गया ‘खेला’, भाजपा ने दोहरा शतक लगाकर दर्ज की ऐतिहासिक जीत, अपनी सीट भी नहीं बचा पाई ममता बनर्जी
